theme: regulator, court, enforcement, and company-notice leads
goal: Upgrade one U.S. court-facing LIMEN row beyond summary-only or title-only normalization without using credentialed or paid court access.

priority_cases.task: OCR the locally captured DOJ-linked indictment PDF to recover exact caption and case-number metadata from the document body.
expected_outputs: Machine-readable patch plan for shared ledgers

safe_resource_limits.network: none required after local inputs are present

priority_cases.task: Run a bounded public-entrypoint verification pass for one open court-facing complaint decree order or docket artifact without credentialed PACER access.
expected_outputs: One open court-facing document URL if reachable without login
If still blocked, a stricter barrier note distinguishing official login gates from public-mirror friction
Machine-readable fields for caption case number and procedural posture if success occurs

safe_resource_limits.network: small bounded probes only; no bulk crawling

paper_thesis_use: Hostile-reviewer methods note on why court-family thinness splits into access-constrained versus compute-constrained blockers.
Dashboard facet separating agency-summary-only rows, document-artifact-captured rows, and exact-court-anchor rows.
Thesis reproducibility discussion on public-record normalization limits.
success_condition: At least one U.S. row advances from blocker-only prose to exact court-anchor metadata or to a sharply evidenced blocker class with machine-readable next steps.
boundary_notes: No PACER login, fees, credentials, sealed records, or private attachments.
Keep posture-sensitive wording; do not convert allegations or agency summaries into adjudicated facts.
